questions.question: If the polynomial $p(x) = x^4 - 6x^3 + 16x^2 - 25x + 10$ is divided by another polynomial $g(x) = x^2 - 2x + k$, the remainder is found to be of the form $x + a$. Find the values of $k$ and $a$.

steps: Perform long division of $p(x)$ by $g(x) = x^2 - 2x + k$.
Subtract $x^2(x^2 - 2x + k)$ from $p(x)$ to get the first partial remainder, then bring down terms to continue.
Continue the division until the degree of the remainder is less than the degree of the divisor ($2$).
Equate the calculated remainder $(k - 9)x + (10 - k(4 - k))$ to the given remainder $(1 \cdot x + a)$ and solve for $k$ and $a$ by comparing coefficients.
solution.derivation: $x^4 - 6x^3 + 16x^2 - 25x + 10 = (x^2 - 2x + k)(x^2 - 4x + (8 - k)) + (k - 9)x + (10 - 4k + k^2 - 8k) \\ \text{Comparing remainder } (k - 9)x + (10 - 12k + k^2) \text{ with } x + a \\ k - 9 = 1 \Rightarrow k = 10 \text{ (Correction: } k - 9 = 1 \Rightarrow k = 10 \text{ is wrong, let us re-evaluate)} \\ \text{Correct division: } x^2(x^2 - 2x + k) = x^4 - 2x^3 + kx^2 \\ \text{Remainder: } -4x^3 + (16-k)x^2 - 25x + 10 \\ -4x(x^2 - 2x + k) = -4x^3 + 8x^2 - 4kx \\ \text{Remainder: } (8-k)x^2 + (4k-25)x + 10 \\ (8-k)(x^2 - 2x + k) = (8-k)x^2 - 2(8-k)x + k(8-k) \\ \text{Equating remainder coefficients to } 1x + a: \\ (4k - 25) - (-16 + 2k) = 1 \Rightarrow 2k - 9 = 1 \Rightarrow 2k = 10 \Rightarrow k = 5 \\ a = 10 - k(8 - k) = 10 - 5(8 - 5) = 10 - 15 = -5$
